title: Ports, Immutable Values, Promise Ownership, and Lifecycle · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for ports, immutable values, promise ownership, and lifecycle and accepts only compiler plus runtime changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named network, DNS, browser, accessibility, security, load, legal-review, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for explicitly owned crawler component graph. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice…
objectives: Direct policy, fetch, robots, parse, extract, frontier, render, audit, storage, and report dependencies through narrow typed ports.
Create readonly branded identities only after unknown runtime values pass current validation and normalization.
Assign each Promise a caller, settlement path, concurrency slot, cancellation rule, error destination, and shutdown consequence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Directed Ports · changed case · Quiz Directed Ports

quiz case — A quiz TypeScript team handles case 8022 during a narrow-screen keyboard and screen-reader review. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove port direction, admitted values, Promise and AbortSignal ownership, and handle-clean lifecycle; release evidence binds source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, lockfile, package, browser revision, container, configuration, and restored state; then reorder one frontier, p-limit, Promise, AbortSignal, stream, retry, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only.
